cases.passage: In a recent cricket match, Rohan, an opening batsman, was struggling to score runs. He had eaten a light breakfast consisting of oats and fruits but had not had lunch yet. As the match progressed, he started to feel weak and his batting technique suffered. His coach, who was observing from the sidelines, realized that Rohan needed a quick energy boost to perform better. He advised Rohan to consume a banana and a few dates to replenish his energy stores.

questions.q: Why did Rohan's coach advise him to consume a banana and dates during the match?
questions.explanation: The coach advised Rohan to consume a banana and dates to replenish his energy stores because he had not had lunch yet and was feeling weak.

questions.q: What is the primary function of carbohydrates in the human body?
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates are the primary source of energy for the human body, particularly for the brain and muscles.

questions.q: What type of nutrition is essential for an athlete like Rohan to perform at his best?
questions.explanation: All types of nutrition - macro-nutrition (carbohydrates, proteins, fats), micro-nutrition (vitamins, minerals), and hydration - are essential for an athlete like Rohan to perform at his best.

questions.q: Why is it essential for athletes to consume a balanced diet that includes fruits, vegetables, and whole grains?
questions.explanation: A balanced diet that includes fruits, vegetables, and whole grains provides essential nutrients, vitamins, and minerals that help athletes perform at their best, improve their endurance, increase their strength, and enhance their immune system.

questions.q: What is the recommended timing for a light snack or meal for an athlete like Rohan before a match?
questions.explanation: A light snack or meal should be consumed within 1-2 hours before the match to provide a quick energy boost and prevent digestive discomfort during the match.

cases.passage: Amar, a young sprinter, was training intensively for the upcoming national championships. He had been following a strict diet plan that included lean proteins, complex carbohydrates, and healthy fats. However, he was struggling to recover from his intense training sessions and was experiencing muscle soreness and fatigue. His coach recommended that he increase his intake of omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants to aid in recovery.

questions.q: Why was Amar experiencing muscle soreness and fatigue despite his strict diet?
opts: (A) He was not consuming enough calories
(B) He was not consuming enough protein
(C) He was not consuming enough omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants
(D) He was not training hard enough
questions.explanation: Amar was experiencing muscle soreness and fatigue because he was not consuming enough omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants, which are essential for recovery after intense training sessions.

questions.q: What is the primary function of antioxidants in the human body?
questions.explanation: Antioxidants play a crucial role in neutralizing free radicals, which can cause oxidative stress and damage to cells and tissues.

questions.q: Why is it essential for athletes to consume lean proteins as part of their diet?
questions.explanation: Lean proteins are essential for athletes to build and repair muscles, increase endurance, enhance immune function, and aid in recovery after intense training sessions.

questions.q: What is the recommended timing for consuming a post-workout snack or meal for an athlete like Amar?
questions.explanation: A post-workout snack or meal should be consumed within 30 minutes after the workout to aid in recovery and replenish energy stores.

questions.q: What is the role of omega-3 fatty acids in the human body?
opts: (C) To reduce inflammation and aid in recovery
questions.explanation: Omega-3 fatty acids play a crucial role in reducing inflammation and aiding in recovery after intense training sessions and injury.
